price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings. Many nurseries run end-of-season sales after Labor Day.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall, plant in fall. Fall-planted hydrangeas establish stronger root systems over winter and bloom the following spring.

price_seasonality.tip: Order bare-root trees in January for best selection. If buying potted, wait for fall clearance.

price_seasonality.tip: Fall is actually ideal for planting evergreens. Buy in October for best price AND best planting conditions.

price_seasonality.note: Spring demand drives prices up 20-30%. Fall is ideal for buying. Bare-root options in late winter offer the deepest discounts.
price_seasonality.tip: Fall planting gives roots 4-5 months to establish before summer stress. Best value is bare-root in February.

price_seasonality.note: Bare-root roses (January-March) are cheapest but limited selection. Potted roses peak in spring. End-of-season clearance offers 20-40% off.
price_seasonality.tip: For Knock Out roses, fall planting works well in zones 6-9. Bare-root in late winter is cheapest.

price_seasonality.note: Spring is peak demand. Most nurseries stock blueberries February-May only. Fall clearance saves 15-25%.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy 2-3 varieties for cross-pollination. Spring planting gives the best first-year fruit production.

price_seasonality.note: Flowering trees sell out fast in spring. Fall planting is underrated and often 15-20% cheaper.
price_seasonality.tip: If a specific cultivar matters, buy in March before sellout. If price matters more, wait for fall.

price_seasonality.note: Bare-root fruit trees (January-March) are 30-50% cheaper than potted, but selection sells out fast. Fall clearance on potted trees offers good value.
price_seasonality.tip: Order bare-root trees in January for best selection. If buying potted, wait for fall clearance.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings as nurseries clear summer inventory.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for the best deals. Liriope is extremely tough and establishes quickly even when planted in autumn.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings. Ajuga is often available in flats for better per-plant pricing.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall or look for flat pricing in spring. Ajuga spreads aggressively, so fewer plants cover more ground than you might expect.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Fall clearance offers savings, though selection may be limited for smaller groundcover varieties.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in spring for best selection or fall for clearance pricing. Creeping thyme is drought-tolerant once established and makes an excellent lawn alternative.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings. Sedum divisions and plugs offer more affordable coverage for large areas.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for the best deals. Sedum is extremely drought-tolerant and nearly indestructible once established.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when landscaping demand surges. Fall is the best time to buy evergreens as nurseries clear inventory before winter.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for clearance pricing and ideal planting conditions. Evergreen junipers establish best when planted before the ground freezes.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Fall is an excellent time to buy as the dramatic pink plumes are on full display and nurseries offer clearance pricing.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in early fall when the pink plumes are visible so you can see the quality. Fall planting gives roots time to establish before winter dormancy.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when warm-season grasses break dormancy and demand surges. Fall clearance offers the best deals. Often sold as an annual in colder zones, which keeps prices competitive.
price_seasonality.tip: In zones 9-11 where it is perennial, buy in early spring for the full growing season. In colder zones, treat as a seasonal annual and look for late-spring deals.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings. Hameln is one of the most popular fountain grass cultivars, so availability is usually good.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for the best deals. Fall-planted fountain grass establishes stronger root systems over winter and fills out faster the following spring.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Fall clearance offers savings. Blue Fescue is often sold in multi-packs for better per-plant pricing on mass plantings.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for clearance pricing. Blue Fescue is a cool-season grass that actually prefers fall planting, establishing best before summer heat arrives.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand surges. Fall is the best time to buy as nurseries clear large-container inventory. Pampas Grass commands higher prices due to its dramatic size.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for clearance pricing. Spring planting is ideal in zones 7-8 where winters are cooler, giving the plant a full season to establish before its first winter.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Astilbe is a shade garden staple with steady demand. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings as nurseries clear inventory.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for the best deals. Astilbe establishes well when fall-planted and rewards with blooms the following summer. Bareroot divisions are the most economical option.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Heuchera's colorful foliage drives premium pricing for newer cultivars. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall when nurseries discount foliage plants. Heuchera is semi-evergreen in mild climates, so fall planting gives roots time to establish before winter.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Fringed bleeding heart (D. eximia) reblooms through fall unlike common bleeding heart. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for clearance pricing. Fringed bleeding heart is more heat-tolerant than common bleeding heart and blooms much longer, making it the better value.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Magnus is the most widely grown purple coneflower cultivar. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall, plant in fall. Fall-planted coneflowers establish stronger root systems and bloom prolifically the following summer. Seeds from spent flowers attract goldfinches.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Happy Returns is one of the most popular reblooming daylilies. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings. Bareroot fans offer the best per-plant value.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy bareroot fans in late winter or containerized plants in fall for the best pricing. Happy Returns reblooms all season, so you get more bloom time per dollar than single-flush daylilies.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Bee balm spreads readily, so fewer plants cover more ground than expected. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall or start with fewer plants than you think you need. Bee balm colonizes quickly via underground runners. Divide every 3 years to maintain vigor and share with friends.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Walker's Low is the 2007 Perennial Plant of the Year, so it commands steady demand. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for clearance pricing. Shear plants after the first flush of blooms for a strong rebloom in late summer. One of the lowest-maintenance perennials available.

price_seasonality.note: Prices peak in spring when demand is highest. Russian sage is sometimes priced as a small shrub due to its woody base and large size. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for the best deals. Russian sage thrives in poor, dry soil, so skip the expensive amended beds. One of the most drought-tolerant perennials available.

price_seasonality.note: October Glory is a popular, reliably colorful Red Maple cultivar. Bare-root availability is limited - most retailers sell containerized. Fall clearance offers the best deals on potted trees.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall when inventory is clearing and planting conditions are ideal. Mature October Glorys can reach 50 ft, so site placement matters more than price.

price_seasonality.note: Heritage is the most popular River Birch cultivar thanks to its heat tolerance and resistance to bronze birch borer. Often sold as single-trunk or clump form - clump form is typically 20-30% more expensive.
price_seasonality.tip: Decide on single-trunk vs clump form before shopping - prices vary significantly. Fall planting is ideal for this moisture-loving tree.

price_seasonality.note: Red Sunset is widely considered the best Red Maple cultivar for fall color reliability. Often priced slightly higher than October Glory due to demand. Fall clearance is the best time to buy.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall when inventory is clearing. Red Sunset colors up earlier than most Red Maples, so you get a longer fall display.

price_seasonality.note: Sweetbay tolerates wet soils that kill most trees, making it a niche-but-valuable option. It is semi-evergreen in the South (zones 8-10) and deciduous in the North. Fall clearance offers the best deals.
price_seasonality.tip: Fall is the best time to plant - the fragrant lemony blooms appear the following spring. Choose sweetbay for wet sites where other magnolias would fail.

price_seasonality.note: Rose of Sharon is sold under many cultivar names - Lucy, Pink Chiffon, Blue Bird, Azurri Blue Satin, Lavender. Prices vary widely by cultivar, with newer patented varieties commanding premium pricing. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for clearance. Standard cultivars (Lucy, Blue Bird) are cheaper than newer patented series like Chiffon or Satin. All bloom prolifically in late summer when few other shrubs do.

bloom_time: Mid to late spring (repeat in summer)

price_seasonality.note: Wine & Roses is a Proven Winners patented cultivar, so prices stay firm at retail. The dark burgundy foliage is the main selling point - it holds color all season. Fall clearance offers the best deals.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for clearance pricing. Full sun produces the darkest, richest foliage color - partial shade causes the burgundy to fade toward green.

price_seasonality.note: Frost Proof is the most cold-hardy Gardenia cultivar, extending the range into zone 7 where most gardenias fail. Prices peak in spring when fragrant-shrub demand is highest. Fall clearance offers 15-30% savings.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for clearance, but plant in spring in zone 7 so the shrub can establish before winter. Afternoon shade in zones 9-11 prevents leaf scorch.

price_seasonality.note: Dwarf Alberta Spruce has a distinct December price bump because landscapers use potted specimens as living Christmas trees. Spring is the main buying season when containers are freshly stocked. Heat-sensitive - not recommended south of zone 6.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for the best deals and ideal planting weather. Avoid hot, dry sites - this conifer dislikes summer heat above zone 6. Watch for spider mites in warm climates.

price_seasonality.note: Autumn Royalty is the largest of the Encore series and won Rhododendron of the Year from the American Rhododendron Society. Deep lavender-purple reblooming flowers make it the most asked-for Encore cultivar - prices stay firm in spring demand. Fall clearance offers the best deals.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for price and planting conditions. Plant with afternoon shade in zones 8-9 to protect reblooming flower buds from heat stress.

price_seasonality.note: Autumn Twist is prized for its bi-color white-and-purple-striped blooms (each flower is unique). Same mid-size growth habit as Autumn Royalty but with showier flowers. Spring is peak demand and peak price; fall clearance offers 15-30% savings.
price_seasonality.tip: The striped flower pattern varies plant to plant - if you care about the look, buy in person or from a retailer with return policies. Fall planting establishes roots before the spring bloom flush.

price_seasonality.note: Autumn Angel is the dwarf white-flowered Encore - only 2-3 ft mature, much smaller than Royalty or Twist. Ideal for small-space foundation plantings or low hedges. Patented PP15227 so prices stay firm at retail; fall clearance is the best time to buy.
price_seasonality.tip: Because of its compact size, Autumn Angel is sold mostly in 1-gal and 3-gal containers. Plant in groups of 3-5 for hedge effect. Fall planting is ideal.

price_seasonality.note: Nandina is a tough evergreen shrub with year-round foliage color (green in summer, red in fall/winter) and red berries. Cultivars range from dwarf 'Firepower' (2-3 ft, nearly fruitless) to standard species (5-8 ft with berries). Flagged as invasive in some southern states - check local regulations. Fall clearance offers 20-30% savings.
price_seasonality.tip: For a low-maintenance colorful hedge, standard Nandina is hard to beat. If you live in the deep South, consider sterile dwarf cultivars instead of the fruiting species to avoid volunteer seedlings.

price_seasonality.note: Santa Rosa is the classic self-pollinating Japanese plum introduced by Luther Burbank in 1906 - still a home-orchard favorite for its crimson skin, amber flesh, and heavy crops. Bare-root trees (January-March) run 30-50% cheaper than potted. Requires 300-500 chill hours so it struggles in the deep South zone 9 without a cold winter.
price_seasonality.tip: Order bare-root in January for best price and selection. Self-fertile so one tree is enough, but planting a second Japanese plum increases yields. Needs full sun and well-drained soil.

price_seasonality.note: Dwarf Cavendish is the most common edible banana worldwide, bred for container growing and small-space yards. Tropical - outdoor year-round only in zones 10-11; in zone 9 it dies back in winter and resprouts from the corm. Can be grown indoors in containers in colder zones. Spring demand drives prices up; fall clearance is the best deal window.
price_seasonality.tip: Buy in fall for clearance, but plant in spring after last frost (zone 9) or anytime (zone 10-11). Plants take 2-3 years to fruit. A potted Dwarf Cavendish can be brought indoors for winter in zone 8 or colder.
